title: Ecotone transition biomes: Coastal Scrub and Treeline
summary: Sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. Coastal Scrub and Treeline are now live in the five-biome chain between beach, forest, and tundra. The remaining work in this packet is the post-wiring science and polish layer: keep the Pacific coastal branch accurate, surface shared-species overlap clearly, harden the live route with better coverage, and finish the post-audit coastal cleanup.

execution_order.goal: Scaffold the Coastal Scrub biome in isolation with assets, biome data, and tests.
execution_order.why_now: Content-first scaffolding keeps the live runtime stable while making the new biome testable.

execution_order.goal: Review Coastal Scrub for science accuracy, gradient readability, and sprite legibility.
execution_order.why_now: Science accuracy is a hard gate and should be checked before Treeline scaffolding.

execution_order.goal: Scaffold the Treeline biome in isolation with assets, biome data, and tests.
execution_order.why_now: Same pattern as Coastal Scrub: content first, wiring later.

execution_order.goal: Review Treeline for science accuracy, gradient readability, and any content-model mismatch that matters once it becomes live.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline introduces lichens and tundra-edge shared species, so one critique pass now is cheaper than fixing drift after travel wiring.

execution_order.goal: Replace the current equal-width journal biome tabs with a selector that can survive the full five-biome chain.
execution_order.why_now: The current three-tab layout will not fit names like Coastal Scrub once the ecotones are wired live.

execution_order.goal: Create a shared-species module or equally clear single-source pattern for species that appear in both parent biomes and ecotones.
execution_order.why_now: This prevents duplicate-content drift and protects journal behavior before the five-biome chain is wired.

execution_order.goal: Wire both ecotones into the world map and live travel runtime.
execution_order.why_now: After both biomes are stable in isolation, connect the five-biome gradient chain.

execution_order.goal: Review the complete five-biome gradient chain.
execution_order.why_now: The travel flow and educational gradient only become clear when the full chain is live.

execution_order.goal: Make the journal follow the focused world-map node when it is opened from the map.
execution_order.why_now: Now that the full route is live, the map and journal need to feel like one connected browsing loop instead of two separate modes.

execution_order.goal: Let the journal surface multi-biome sightings for shared species without creating duplicate entries.
execution_order.why_now: This is where the ecotone lesson starts to feel visible to the player instead of staying hidden in content data.

execution_order.goal: Add a true world-map travel smoke and sync the travel doc to the live content set.
execution_order.why_now: After the journal context and shared-species overlap are settled, the live route should be locked into tests and docs so future agents are not working from stale assumptions.

execution_order.goal: Run an explicit science-accuracy audit across the live biome set after the ecotone chain and journal updates land.
execution_order.why_now: The content surface is larger now, so factual drift should be checked directly instead of assumed away.

execution_order.goal: Fix the remaining Pacific coastal science mismatches surfaced by the audit.
execution_order.why_now: The five-biome route is now structurally sound, so the next best improvement is tightening the last two coastal accuracy issues instead of letting them linger.

guardrails: Science accuracy is a hard gate for every species, fact, and habitat pairing.
Pacific coastal content should use native anchor species unless the game intentionally teaches an introduced species as introduced.
Ecotone zones must read as left-to-right gradients, not arbitrary mixed-species lists.
The five-biome journal selector must stay readable at 192x144 instead of assuming the current three-tab math will hold forever.
Opening the journal from the world map should respect the place the player is actively browsing, not stale doorway-origin state.
Shared species should preserve entry IDs across parent biome and ecotone so the journal deduplicates correctly.
Do not destabilize the live runtime while scaffolding the first ecotone biome.
Keep the build green after each major step.
acceptance_focus: The final ecosystem chain reads as a gradient: beach -> coastal-scrub -> forest -> treeline -> tundra.
Each ecotone teaches a distinct ecological concept through placement and content.
Shared species behave correctly in the journal and discovery flow.
Map browsing, journal context, and travel flow feel like one connected system instead of detached modes.
The Pacific beach-to-scrub branch avoids quietly teaching introduced or mismatched habitat content as if it were the default native pattern.
The new biomes feel like connected habitats, not side-theme levels.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/ecotone-design.md, and this packet. Start with Coastal Scrub as an isolated biome and keep the live runtime stable until both ecotones have tests.
